items.title: Major winemaker threatens to move business overseas if tax changes go through

children.content: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is threatening to move the business internationally in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

gallery.altText: Two people in flurescent vests walking through a vineyard

thumbnail.altText: Two people in flurescent vests walking through a vineyard

items.title: Ex-police officer's messages did not amount to 'implied admissions' of rape, court told

children.content: A woman who says she was raped by a former South Australian police officer did not confront her alleged attacker "because she knew it wasn't true", a court has heard.

image.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

items.title: Major wine retailer puts vineyards up for sale amid industry-wide squeeze

children.content: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

image.altText: Wine bottles on a bottle shop shelf.

items.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager

children.content: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.

image.altText: A man n a blue shirt is standing in shade outside a building with a sign that says Aṉangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara.

items.title: Buses, brekkie and circus skills getting kids through the school gate

children.content: Schools in the outback often struggle to maintain consistent attendance rates, but the Wilcannia community is going the extra mile to get kids into the classroom.

image.altText: Two smiling girls sitting on a bus.

items.title: Aussie diver called in for 'scary as hell' mission to rescue Laos villagers

children.content: A diver describes the "emotional moment" when he found a group of men alive after they were trapped for more than a week in a Laos cave, while a South Australian renowned for his ability to navigate tight spaces joins the rescue mission.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit and orange helmet climbing backwards in a cave.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit and orange helmet climbing backwards in a cave.

items.title: Adelaide man to face court over alleged fuel drive-offs using trucks

children.content: A man has been charged for allegedly using trucks in serial petrol drive-offs north of Adelaide, with police estimating the cost to be more than $8,000.

image.altText: A old truck parked on grass in a backyard next to a fence

items.title: Waste company fined $1.1m over 2014 double-fatal Adelaide truck crash

children.content: Almost 12 years after a Cleanaway truck killed two people and injured two others, the company has been sentenced in South Australia's Supreme Court.

image.altText: A person in full coveralls spraying water on the road next to a sewage tanker truck on its side

items.title: After escaping sexual slavery, Yazidis re-traumatised by return of 'ISIS brides'

children.content: The return of several 'ISIS brides' from Syria has been re-traumatising for Australian Yazidis, many of whom escaped sexual slavery at the hands of the terrorist organisation.

image.altText: A portrait shot of Amera Ali.

items.title: No new evidence in Gus Lamont search, but police 'won't give up'

children.content: A renewed search for missing four-year-old boy Gus Lamont has failed to uncover additional evidence, police reveal.

image.altText: An SA Police officer at Oak Park Station.

items.title: Man joins brother and late mother's reconciliation journey after discovering he is Indigenous

children.content: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

gallery.altText: Two men with arms on each other's shoulders looking at a fire

thumbnail.altText: Two men with arms on each other's shoulders looking at a fire

items.title: 'Lost opportunity' to help air force serviceman before he died, inquest finds

children.content: A coroner has found that the death of an RAAF serviceman could have been prevented, including when he left a military health centre during a mental health crisis.
Warning: This story contains discussion of suicide and mental health issues.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: Modelling suggests 'second wave' of algal bloom in Spencer Gulf

children.content: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

image.altText: A man sits at a table near the beach with a laptop.

items.title: Protesters taken into custody as police enter camp at Olympic stadium site

children.content: Tensions flared at Victoria Park after police, firefighters and Brisbane City Council officers entered a protest camp ahead of the $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.

gallery.altText: A group of police officers walking through Victoria Park

thumbnail.altText: A group of police officers walking through Victoria Park

items.title: Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president

children.content: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.

image.altText: A man stands at a lectern in a suit.

items.title: KPMG boss quits amid auditing scandal

children.content: KPMG Australia chief executive Andrew Yates has stepped down amid a scandal involving audit partners accessing confidential client documents.

image.altText: A bald man in a suit and red tie speaks into a microphone while siting at a desk in a room decorated with timber.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test

children.content: Blue Origin says all personnel are accounted for after experiencing an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

items.title: White House asks Americans to report their neighbours as 'aliens'

children.content: The website features a space-inspired background but its real purpose is to highlight the work of ICE.

image.altText: a screenshot of a white house website that reads "they walk among us"

items.title: Erin Patterson mushroom trial appeal set after 'miscarriage of justice' claim

children.content: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

image.altText: Erin Patterson being led into a prison van

items.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?

children.content: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget.
Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.

items.title: Mouse plague forces return of COVID-style restrictions at country WA schools

children.content: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

image.altText: A mouse in a plastic container full of kids dress-up clothes.

items.title: Surging Blues challenging Cats after Daniher tribute at the MCG

children.content: Neale Daniher is remembered with an impeccably observed minute's silence at headquarters, as Carlton hunts a third win in a row under interim boss Josh Fraser. Follow live.

image.altText: Geelong run through a banner that reads RIP Neale Daniher

items.title: This comedian has 1.7m followers. He doesn't think it's real

children.content: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

gallery.altText: Will Gibb holds a finger to his mouth and gently smiles wearing an orange and maroon crochet shirt against orange backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Will Gibb holds a finger to his mouth and gently smiles wearing an orange and maroon crochet shirt against orange backdrop.

items.title: Growing garlic is one of life's slower lessons

children.content: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

image.altText: Koren holds garlic bulbs in a basket, she's smiling.

avatar.alt: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

items.title: Our critics can't stop listening to these 10 albums from May

children.content: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

image.altText: Collage of Genesis Owusu, Ecca Vandal, Kneecap and Mulga Bore Hard Rock.

items.title: Farnham says he will not sing on stage again ahead of tribute concert

children.content: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a Melbourne tribute concert later this year.

items.title: Award-winning volunteer charged with possessing child-abuse images

children.content: A former police staffer and lauded SES volunteer is granted bail on a charge of possessing child-abuse images.

image.altText: Headshot of man

items.title: Claims about high-profile married man's relationship raised in extortion case

children.content: A court has heard a man charged with extortion was "potentially gagged" by his bail terms in an effort to protect a high-profile married man.

image.altText: 'Cairns Courthouse' written on the side of a building.

items.title: Women's shed ex-board member banned over alleged transphobic comments

children.content: A former board member of a women's shed in Victoria has been evicted after allegedly "spewing" discriminatory and hurtful comments about transgender people.

image.altText: a close up of a shed and a sign that says 'Women's Shed Ballarat'

items.title: Executive defends appointment of 'Pink Ladies' mentee to Sydney council

children.content: A Western Sydney local government executive undermined recruitment processes to hire a woman she mentored for years and socialised with as part of a networking group dubbed the Pink Ladies.

image.altText: Angela has curly short hair, glasses and a dark blazer. She sits in front of a light wall.

items.title: A 'horror night' of airstrikes leaves southern Lebanese city fearing what's next

children.content: New waves of missile strikes and vast advisories from the Israeli government to evacuate have left residents in Lebanon's south uncertain of where to turn.

gallery.altText: A car covered in debris from an airstrike in a residential area.

thumbnail.altText: A car covered in debris from an airstrike in a residential area.

items.title: US prosecutor denies launching criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll

children.content: Chicago's top federal prosecutor has denied his office has launched a criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll, who accused Donald Trump of raping her in the mid-1990s.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: Heatwaves unbearable in UK 'built for a climate that no longer exists'

children.content: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.

image.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.

items.title: Aboriginal families in Federal Court bid for traditional ownership recognition

children.content: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

image.altText: The coastline of a remote beach.

items.title: Socceroos boosted as young midfielder switches allegiance from Italy

children.content: The Socceroos have been boosted ahead of the FIFA World Cup, with young midfielder Cristian Volpato switching allegiance from Italy to Australia.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato jumps and screams with joy after scoring
